LanguageData.Rpt_Set_MedievalTechTip: When enabled, envoys, missionaries and summit delegates depart without a shuttle, reward goods arrive at the map edge instead of by drop pod, international summits no longer require a comms console, and founding a trade company and upgrading settlement buildings no longer require research. Meant for medieval-themed playthroughs. If Medieval Overhaul is installed, this starts enabled by default — you can turn it off at any time.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_MedievalDetected: Medieval Overhaul detected — this setting was enabled by default. Feel free to turn it off if your playthrough spans beyond the medieval era.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_TitlesTip: Changes the displayed names of your own court positions (chancellor, marshal, steward, spymaster). Leave blank to use the default names. Effects are unchanged.
LanguageData.Rpt_Set_NotorietyTip: Destroying settlements builds notoriety; past the threshold, factions band together to hunt your colony. Turning this off stops accrual and dissolves any ongoing hunt.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_UltAlly: You cannot send an ultimatum to an ally.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_UltGlobalCooldown: The stir from your last ultimatum has not settled. ({0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_Marry_Cooldown: Marriage talks are still fresh. ({0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_Marry_OnceReign: A marriage has already been arranged under this leader. (available again after succession)
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterMarryFloorBroken: After repeated hostile acts, {0} has turned its back. The marriage no longer shields your relations — from now on they can collapse all the way.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_RenewNotYet: Too early to renew. (available in {0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCoalInvite: {0} is raising a coalition to check the hegemon {1}, and reaches out to us.\n\nAccept, and the coalition forms — its members will send reinforcements when the hegemon raids you. Refuse, and this coalition falls apart, disappointing its leader.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_TruceIndepWar: No ceasefire during a war of independence.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_TruceHunt: No ceasefire with a faction hunting your colony.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_TruceJointWar: No ceasefire with the target of your joint war.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_ReparationConfirm: Send {1} silver in reparations to {0}.\n\nRelations stay hostile, but trust builds by +{2}. Accumulated trust paves the way to a ceasefire and eventual reconciliation.
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_EmbargoCooldown: The embargo was lifted too recently. ({0} days)
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CourtNoLeaderRole: No one holds the leader role.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CourtNoCandidate: No colonist is available for appointment.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CourtTipLeader: Head of the court. The colonist holding the Ideology leader role is shown automatically. While a leader reigns, notoriety fades faster.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CourtTipChancellor: Oversees diplomacy. Trust gain +{0}% (scales with Social skill)
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CourtTipMarshal: Commands the military. Allied reinforcement size +{0}% (scales with Shooting/Melee skill)
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CourtTipSteward: Manages trade routes. Route income +{0}%, accident chance -{1}% (scales with Construction skill)
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCourtVacated: The {0} position at court is now vacant. ({1})
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_FoundConfirm: Spend {0} silver to found an intelligence bureau. Construction takes {1} days.\n\nOnce founded, the spymaster position becomes available and you can run intelligence operations against other factions.
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_NeedStationed: A spy must be embedded there
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCounterTheft: Someone rifled through the lab and stole research materials for '{0}'. Progress dropped by {1} points.\n\nThe culprit left no trace. A stronger counterintelligence network at the bureau can block or catch such intruders.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCounterSabotage: Someone tampered with the wiring and short-circuited {0} conduits. Batteries discharged and sparks flew.\n\nThe culprit left no trace. A stronger counterintelligence network at the bureau can block or catch such intruders.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCounterCaught: Our counterintelligence network caught a spy sent by {0}. For the next {1} days, no faction will dare send another.\n\nWhat should we do with the captured spy?
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCounterRansom: {0} paid {1} silver in ransom for their spy.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCounterDenounce: We publicly denounced the espionage of {0}. We gain a just cause for {1} days.
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_UpgradeConfirm: Spend {1} silver to upgrade the bureau to tier {0}. Work takes {2} days.\n\nHigher tiers let you maintain more spies.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgSpyEmbedded: Spy {0} is now embedded in {1}. Their hidden numbers are revealed.
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_NoTech: There is no technology to learn from that faction
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgSpyDeployed: Spy {0} has set out for {1}. Infiltration takes {2} days.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSpyFateEscaped: Captured spy: {0} (Lv.{1}) — barely escaped and returned.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CourtTipSpy:
Spymaster (Intellectual)

Spy detection risk reduced by {0}%
Assassination, sabotage and bribe bonus +{1}%p
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_OpBusyFaction: This spy is already running an operation
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_CivilWarActive: Another civil war is already raging
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_InciteNotRipe: The target is not unstable enough (needs power below {0} and 2+ settlements)
LanguageData.Rpt_Act_TargetCivilWar: The target is in a civil war
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_OpTechConfirm: Steal technology from {0} ({1} silver, {2} days).\n\nSuccess is guaranteed, but each day of the operation carries about a {3}% risk of discovery. If discovered, the operation fails and the spy may be executed ({4}% death chance). On success, one unlearned research of that faction\'s era advances greatly — with luck, it completes outright.
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_OpInciteConfirm: Stir up dissidents inside {0} ({1} silver, {2} days).\n\nSuccess is guaranteed, but each day of the operation carries about a {3}% risk of discovery. If discovered, the operation fails and the spy may be executed ({4}% death chance). On success, a civil war breaks out in that faction.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgBureauDone: The intelligence bureau is now tier {0}.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgSpyOpAborted: Spy {0}'s operation was aborted due to the target's circumstances; its cost was recovered.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgSpyInciteOk: The spark planted in {0} has caught fire.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSpyIntro: The inner workings of other factions — their national power, submission pressure and the like — can no longer be seen from the outside.\n\nFound an intelligence bureau from your own settlement\'s screen in the diplomacy tab, train spies and embed them in other factions. While a spy remains embedded, that faction\'s hidden numbers stay revealed.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSpyTechOk: We smuggled out technical documents from {0}. Research '{1}' advanced by {2} points.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSpyTechExposed: Our agent was caught stealing technology from {0}. Relations and trust have suffered badly.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSpyBreakSuffix: Furious, they tore up {0} treaties with us.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSpyInciteExposed: Our agent was caught inciting rebellion in {0}. They are furious and may retaliate.
LanguageData.Rpt_CantSign_CivilWar: Diplomacy is impossible during their civil war
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CivilWarStatus: Civil war — no diplomacy · about {0} days left
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_CivilWarBlocked: No diplomatic actions are possible during a civil war.
LanguageData.Rpt_Spy_AlreadyPlanted: A spy is already embedded there
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterSpyStationExposed: Our spy embedded in {0} was discovered and pulled out. Relations have suffered, and you cannot re-infiltrate for {1} days.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_SubmitTipHidden: Submission pressure ? / {0}\n\nTheir inner workings are unknown. An intelligence bureau's gathering operation can reveal them.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_DemandConfirmBlind: Demand tribute from {0}.\n\nYou are making this demand blind, without knowing their inner workings. If refused, relations suffer badly and you cannot demand again for a while.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWar: Civil war has broken out in {0}. Rebels have risen and the nation is split in two, fighting itself.\n\nUntil it ends, no diplomacy is possible with this faction and no traders will come.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWarProg1Gov: News from the civil war in {0}. Government forces are holding the key strongholds and gaining the upper hand.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWarProg1Reb: News from the civil war in {0}. The rebels' momentum is alarming — province after province is falling into their hands.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWarProg2Gov: The civil war in {0} is nearing its end. Government forces are closing in on the rebel leadership; suppression seems imminent.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWarProg2Reb: The civil war in {0} is nearing its end. The rebels have reached the gates of the capital. Rumor has it the regime will not hold.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWarQuell: The civil war in {0} has ended. Government forces crushed the rebellion, and the nation is slowly regaining stability.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWarCoup: The civil war in {0} ended in a coup. The old leadership was driven out and the mantle has passed to {1}.\n\nThe court has been swept clean — diplomacy must be rebuilt.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCivilWarSplit: The civil war in {0} came to the worst possible end. The nation has been torn in two: the rebels declared independence as {1} and are now at war with their former homeland.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterAsylum: {0}, former {2} of {1}, was purged in the coup and now begs asylum at our settlement. Taking them in gains a colonist, but the new regime will resent it.\n\nNotable skills: {3}
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgAsylumJoined: {0} has been granted asylum and joined the colony.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterRouteToll: Highwaymen are demanding a toll on the trade route to {1}. Pay {2} silver and this quarter's income ({3} silver) arrives untouched. Refuse, and this quarter's income is lost. The route itself remains open either way.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgRouteTollRefused: The toll was refused — this quarter's income from the {0} trade route is lost.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterRouteBoom: The markets of {1} are booming, and the trade route with {0} has brought in an unusually large income of {2} silver.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterRouteDisrupt: The trade route to {1} has been temporarily disrupted, and no income arrived this quarter. Business should return to normal next quarter.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterVisitOffer: {1}, {2} of {0}, wishes to visit our settlement in person. If we accept, they will arrive shortly and stay for {3} day(s). How we host them will shape their personal favor and their faction's trust.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgVisitBusy: A state visit is already underway.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgVisitComing: {0} of {1} will arrive at the settlement soon.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterVisitHost: {0} of {1} has arrived at the settlement. While staying, they live as an honored guest — they follow orders but do no work — and depart after a day.\n\nHow shall we host them? A grand feast costs {2} silver.
LanguageData.Rpt_VisitQuestDesc: {0} of {1} is staying at our settlement as an honored guest. As a guest, they cannot be put to work. Sending them home safely will help relations.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgVisitCold: {0} takes the cold reception as an insult and returns to {1}.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterVisitEnd: {0} has concluded a pleasant visit and returned to {1}. The visit left a fine impression on relations between our peoples.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterVisitDeath: {0}, the state guest under our protection, has died at our settlement. {1} holds us responsible for their death.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterVisitMourn: {0}, our state guest, was killed in the chaos of the attack on our settlement. {1} understands the circumstances and casts little blame, but condolences are owed.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgVisitAborted: {0} cuts the visit short amid the sudden turn of events and returns to {1}.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgVisitStranded: {0} has nowhere left to return to and stays at the settlement.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent0: An envoy of {0} left deeply offended by a blunder on our side. Left alone, relations will sour.\n\nSend an envoy with apologies to smooth it over — or, if you have a chancellor, entrust them with mediation.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent1: Rumors are spreading through the settlements of {0} that we speak ill of them behind their backs. Whoever started them, left alone they will harden into fact.\n\nSend an envoy to set the record straight — or, if you have a chancellor, let them talk the rumors down.
LanguageData.Rpt_CourtEvent1_Pay: Send an envoy to clarify ({0} silver)
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent2: A delegation of {0} has arrived unannounced, expecting a reception befitting their standing. How we receive them will travel far.\n\nHost them generously — or, if you have a chancellor, receive them with grace at no cost.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent3: {0} is picking at the wording of an old agreement, pressing an interpretation that favors them.\n\nPay for a renegotiation to settle it — or, if you have a chancellor, refute them point by point.
LanguageData.Rpt_CourtEvent3_Role: The chancellor argues the letter of the law ({0})
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent4: An armed band from {0} is flexing its muscle near the settlement and demanding tribute.\n\nPay them off — or, if you have a marshal, answer strength with strength. Ignore them at your own risk.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent5: An armed band of deserters from {0} is prowling the edge of our territory. Sooner or later they will eye our stores.\n\nHire mercenaries to drive them off — or, if you have a marshal, mount a punitive show of force.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent6: {0} proposes a joint military exercise — and clearly expects a show of good faith in supplies.\n\nContribute supplies — or, if you have a marshal, show good faith in person. Refusing will disappoint them.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent7: {0} protests that weapons traced back to us were used in crimes on their territory.\n\nPay compensation to settle it — or, if you have a marshal, trace the weapons and clear our name. Stonewalling will sour relations.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent8: A caravan of {0} is demanding compensation over damaged cargo and unpaid dues.\n\nPay the compensation — or, if you have a steward, entrust them with mediation. Refusing will sour relations.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent9: A caravan of {0} claims our scales are rigged. If the story spreads, other traders will turn away too.\n\nPay for a certified re-weighing — or, if you have a steward, prove our honesty with a public inspection.
LanguageData.Rpt_CourtEvent9_Role: The steward holds a public inspection ({0})
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent10: Consigned cargo belonging to {0} was lost in an accident at our warehouse, and they are holding us liable.\n\nPay full compensation — or, if you have a steward, salvage and restore to limit the damage. Refusing outright will badly sour relations.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterCourtEvent11: {0} has announced a toll increase on our merchants passing through their territory.\n\nAccept and pay — or, if you have a steward, negotiate the increase away. Mere protest will sour relations.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCourtEventPaid: The matter with {0} was quietly settled.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCourtEventRaidIgnored: The demand from {0} was ignored. Time will tell how they respond.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCourtEventRoleOk0: {0}'s words carried the day — the matter with {1} became a chance to build trust.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCourtEventRoleOk1: {0} settled the matter by strength — {1} won't press the issue again.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCourtEventRoleOk2: {0} squared the accounts cleanly and earned the trust of {1}.
LanguageData.Rpt_MsgCourtEventBotched: {0}'s clumsy handling only made things worse — {1} is angrier than before.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterVisitMarry: During the stay, {0} of {2} has fallen for our colonist {1}. If the marriage is blessed, {0} will remain at the settlement as {1}'s spouse, tying our colony to {2} by blood.\n\nAs the heart chose freely, no dowry is asked.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterEcoBoom: The world market has entered a boom. Traders arrive more often with heavier purses, and trade route and tribute income rise.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterEcoRecession: The world market has fallen into recession. Traders come less often, trade route income shrinks, and mishaps grow more frequent.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterEcoRecover: The world market has returned to normal.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_ExpoPrepDays: Expo in preparation — opens in {0} days
LanguageData.Rpt_Expo_SummitBusy: An international summit is in progress.
LanguageData.Rpt_Expo_NeedCompany: Requires a trade company of level {0} or higher.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterExpoOpen: The international trade expo has opened. {0} factions are participating: {1}\n\nWhile it lasts you can request goods from participants without the usual markup, and buy from their traders at a discount.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterExpoFail: No faction agreed to attend, and the expo fell through. Part of the cost ({0} silver) was refunded.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterExpoEnd: The international trade expo has closed successfully. Trust with the {0} participating factions has deepened.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterExpoInvite:
{0} is hosting an international trade expo and has invited us. It runs for {1} days.
Joining lets us request goods from the host and participants and trade on favorable terms for the duration of the expo. Decide within 24 hours.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterExpoJoined: You took part in {0}'s expo. For its duration you can request goods from the host and participants and trade on favorable terms.
LanguageData.Rpt_Expo_OtherHosting: {0} is hosting an expo — {1} days left
LanguageData.Rpt_Msg_ExpoVisitBack: {0} has returned from the trade expo.
LanguageData.Rpt_LetterExpoBetray: Our colonists attacked or arrested a guest at the trade expo. The expo collapsed at once, and every participating faction turned hostile. Word of this atrocity spread across the world, and trust and goodwill with all factions plummeted.
LanguageData.Rpt_TT_Recession: Recession — actually Lv.{0}, but treated one tier lower (one route halted, income reduced). It reverts once the economy recovers.
LanguageData.Rpt_Msg_ExpoParty: {1} party guests from {0} have arrived at the expo.
LanguageData.Rpt_Msg_ExpoPartyStart: An expo party has begun. Colonists gather to mingle with the guests.
LanguageData.Rpt_Msg_ExpoVisitCancel: {0} did not board the shuttle, so the expo visit was cancelled.
LanguageData.Rpt_UI_PickupBusy: A shuttle dispatch is already underway. Try again once it finishes.
LanguageData.Rpt_Expo_OtherBusy: Another faction is hosting an expo. You can host one after it ends.
